judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports cover the factual findings (a,b,c) equally well with strong evidence; Report 2 is more decisive on the AI-attribution footer (clearly says "Remove it"), while Report 1 hedges and defers that call to the document owner, making Report 2 slightly stronger overall.

report_1.evidence: **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (lines 22–26)** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `/visits` endpoint returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. ... **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30)** — false. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." ... This entire section must be removed until the feature ships. ... correct it to "available, opt-in per clinic" or remove it.

report_2.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... remove or rewrite as opt-in. **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented.** `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment. ... Remove this section.

report_1.evidence: `before_scheduling.png` shows the scheduling calendar surface ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), but `after_scheduling.png` shows the billing screen ("BILLING / INVOICES"). The capture manifest confirms this isn't just a mislabeled render — `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before image came from `/schedule`.

report_2.evidence: `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES" — the wrong page entirely. The capture manifest confirms this: `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before image came from `/schedule`.

report_1.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **no — file does not exist**; `captures.json` explicitly says "expected capture; no corresponding file". ... one file exists that nothing references: `img/unused_capture_07.png`, which renders as a "PATIENT LIST" screen. It is not in `captures.json` and not used by the doc — it's an orphan

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **No — file does not exist** ... Conversely, `img/unused_capture_07.png` (a "PATIENT LIST" screenshot) exists on disk but is referenced nowhere

report_1.evidence: it should be confirmed with the document owner rather than shipped by default; at minimum the word "draft" must not appear in the sent version.

report_2.evidence: the footer on the last line: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." That is an internal drafting note, not customer content... Remove it.
